en.extract:
The crimson-naped woodpecker, crimson-breasted woodpecker or scarlet-breasted woodpecker (Dryobates cathpharius) is a species of bird in the family Picidae. It is found in Bhutan, China, India, Myanmar and Nepal. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests and subtropical or tropical moist montane forests. It was previously considered conspecific with necklaced woodpecker.


Taxonomy

The taxonomy of this species is somewhat confused. D. cathpharius is sometimes included with 
Dendrocopos pernyii as a single species, but the two have various amounts of red on head and breast and other distinguishing features, including size. In their Handbook of the Birds of the World Alive, del Hoyo, J. et al. recognises them as separate species. Molecular analysis puts both close to D. minor and D. pubescens. There are three subspecies; D. c. cathpharius from western and central Nepal to Arunachal Pradesh in northeastern India; D. c. ludlowi from southeastern Tibet; and D. c. pyrrhothorax from Meghalaya, Nagaland, Manipur, Mizoram and western Myanmar.


Description
The mantle, back and wings are black, with some white spotting and light barring on the wings and one larger white patch. The upper side of the tail is black with white barring on the outer feathers, and the underside is barred in black and white. The chin and throat are white, the central breast is red and the rest of the breast and belly are white or buff streaked with black, with a varying amount of red or pink near the vent. The forehead is white and the crown black, and the male has a red patch on the nape. A black streak extends from the malar region to the nape before continuing to the sides of the chest.


References

The '''crimson-naped woodpecker''', '''crimson-breasted woodpecker''' or '''scarlet-breasted woodpecker''' ('''''Dryobates cathpharius''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Picidae]]. It is found in [[Bhutan]], [[China]], [[India]], [[Myanmar]] and [[Nepal]]. Its natural [[habitat]]s are subtropical or tropical moist lowland [[forest]]s and subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]]s. It was previously considered [[conspecific]] with [[necklaced woodpecker]].

==Taxonomy==
[[file:Crimson-breasted Woodpecker Khangchendzonga NP West Sikkim India 23.04.2016.jpg|thumb|From [[Khangchendzonga National Park]], [[West Sikkim]], India|left]]
The taxonomy of this species is somewhat confused. ''D. cathpharius'' is sometimes included with 
''[[Dendrocopos pernyii]]'' as a single species, but the two have various amounts of red on head and breast and other distinguishing features, including size. In their ''Handbook of the Birds of the World Alive'', ''del Hoyo, J. et al.'' recognises them as separate species. [[Molecular phylogenetics|Molecular analysis]] puts both close to ''[[Dendrocopos minor|D. minor]]'' and ''[[downy woodpecker|D. pubescens]]''. There are three [[subspecies]]; ''D. c. cathpharius'' from western and central Nepal to [[Arunachal Pradesh]] in northeastern India; ''D. c. ludlowi'' from southeastern Tibet; and ''D. c. pyrrhothorax'' from [[Meghalaya]], [[Nagaland]], [[Manipur]], [[Mizoram]] and western Myanmar.<ref name=HBWA>{{cite journal |url=http://www.hbw.com/node/56221 |title=Scarlet-breasted woodpecker (''Dryobates cathpharius'') |author=Winkler, H. |author2=Christie, D.A. |year=2017 |journal=Handbook of the Birds of the World Alive |publisher=Lynx Edicions, Barcelona |doi=10.2173/bow.crbwoo3.01 |s2cid=242712678 |access-date=11 May 2017|url-access=subscription }}</ref>

==Description==
The mantle, back and wings are black, with some white spotting and light barring on the wings and one larger white patch. The upper side of the tail is black with white barring on the outer feathers, and the underside is barred in black and white. The chin and throat are white, the central breast is red and the rest of the breast and belly are white or buff streaked with black, with a varying amount of red or pink near the vent. The forehead is white and the crown black, and the male has a red patch on the nape. A black streak extends from the malar region to the nape before continuing to the sides of the chest.<ref name=Gorman>{{cite book |title=Woodpeckers of the World: A Photographic Guide |last=Gorman |first=Gerard |year=2014 |publisher=Firefly Books |isbn=978-1-77085-309-6 |pages=246–247 }}</ref>

zh.extract:
赤胸啄木鸟（学名：Dryobates cathpharius）为啄木鸟科的鸟类。该物种的模式产地在印度。


亚种
赤胸啄木鸟指名亚种（学名：Picoides cathpharius cathpharius）。分布于中國西藏、尼泊爾中西部與印度東北的阿魯納恰爾邦。该物种的模式产地在印度。
赤胸啄木鸟西藏亚种（学名：Picoides cathpharius ludlowi）。分布於雅魯藏布江流域。该物种的模式产地在西藏。
赤胸啄木鸟東南亚种（学名：Picoides cathpharius pyrrhothorax）。分布于若開山一帶。
